index.aboutParrafo1: JASFLY S.A. is an Argentine company specialising in aerial operations with helicopters and high-precision remote sensing         sensors. Our experience encompasses high-resolution mapping projects for provincial governments, municipalities, environmental agencies, and energy sector companies.
index.aboutParrafo2: We have strategic alliances with leading technology providers in Europe and the Americas, which ensures access to state-of-the-art equipment and international operating standards.
index.aboutParrafo3: Our projects include: generation of urban and rural digital twins, environmental risk assessment and territorial planning, infrastructure surveys, cadastre and tax enforcement, and development of thematic cartography and support for Geographic Information Systems (GIS) for government agencies.
index.aboutParrafo4: We believe that digitalisation based on high-precision georeferenced information is the foundation for understanding, designing, and solving the challenges of our reality.
index.aboutParrafo5: JASFLY has been offering LiDAR technology survey services to various sectors and industries for over three years. We continuously optimise delivery times through advanced automation and cutting-edge processing techniques.
index.aboutParrafo6: But our proposition goes beyond data collection and processing: we apply advanced analytics to transform raw data into actionable information that helps our clients make better decisions.
index.aboutParrafo7: Whether planning infrastructure, managing natural resources, or responding to emergencies, LiDAR technology provides the precision and efficiency needed to act with safety and confidence.

about.empresaTitulo: Technology, experience, and precision at the service of aerial surveying
about.empresaIntro: A company specialized in helicopter operations with an extensive national track record.
about.flotaParrafo: Our state-of-the-art helicopter fleet and the most advanced technology allow us to offer tailored solutions for each of our clients.
about.filmacionesParrafo: With extensive experience in aerial filming, in 2021 JASFLY took a step forward by acquiring a LiDAR system from RIEGL, the world leader in laser systems.
about.equipoParrafo1: Our work is guaranteed by a highly trained team of professionals with experience in different types of projects. In each project we strive to follow and comply with the guidelines of the American Society for Photogrammetry and Remote Sensing (ASPRS) standards.
about.equipoParrafo2: We are proud to be the only company in Argentina with a laser scanner specifically designed for helicopters, allowing us to offer survey services with the highest precision, resolution and versatility.
about.lista1: The only company in Argentina with a specialized laser scanner for helicopters.
about.lista2: Surveys with the highest standards of precision, resolution and versatility.
about.lista3: Digitalization based on high-precision georeferenced data.
about.equipoParrafo3: We believe that digitalization based on high-precision georeferenced data forms the fundamental foundation for understanding, designing, and solving the complex challenges of our reality.
about.equipoCierre: This team and approach aligned across five typical project management phases (initiation, planning, execution, monitoring, and closure) ensure that every JASFLY project is executed with rigor, transparency, and quality.

team.m1Bio: Owner and director of Ecodyma S.A. and JASFLY S.A., with extensive experience in aviation and business management focused on aerial services and infrastructure project development. Strong leadership, decision-making and operations management skills across key sectors. Helicopter pilot, certified for night flights, firefighting, instrument flight on B407, B206, B412 and R22.
team.m2Bio: Aeronautical engineer with over five years of experience in geomatics, specialized in the planning and execution of aerial photogrammetry and LiDAR projects. Has worked with various platforms including fixed-wing aircraft, helicopters and drones, and advanced sensors including ultra-high resolution RGB cameras, multispectral cameras and LiDAR systems. Focused on strategic project planning with airborne sensors and geospatial data processing.
team.m3Bio: Commercial Helicopter Pilot. Helicopter Flight Instructor. Private Fixed-Wing Pilot. License: 75.344. Senior Technician in Air Safety and Operations. Diploma in Air Transport Safety.
team.m4Bio: Specialist in topography and geodesy with over 20 years of experience in civil works and geospatial survey projects. Throughout his career he has developed solid expertise in the measurement and establishment of geodetic networks, construction control and high-precision topographic surveys, using both total stations and GNSS systems.

services.servicesParrafo1: At JASFLY we understand how the places of our world connect, and we explore them from the sky.
services.servicesParrafo2: We work daily alongside engineers, surveyors, architects and consultants to add geospatial value to every project. Backed by our technology, knowledge and experience, we deliver high-quality, timely and cost-effective solutions.
services.servicesParrafo3: Our capabilities support the full project lifecycle: we integrate geospatial data into useful and actionable information, laying solid foundations for decision-making and continuous monitoring.
services.topografia2: Generation of Digital Elevation Models (DEM), Terrain Models (DTM), vegetation and structure height models (CHM or nDSM), and relative elevation models (REM).
services.vectorizacion3: Sewage, street lighting, and other infrastructure vectorization.
services.geologia2: Hydrological and hydraulic studies and simulations.
services.flujoIntro: At JASFLY we know that the success of a geospatial survey depends not only on the technology, but also on a structured and rigorous methodology. From the initial definition stage to the delivery of the final report, each phase is designed to ensure precision, quality, and fulfillment of objectives. Our workflow combines detailed planning, quality control, and professionalism, ensuring that the results meet the project requirements.
services.tab1Item1: Kickoff meeting with the client to validate scope, expected accuracy, acceptance criteria, and schedule.
services.tab1Item2: Establishment of the communication plan and risk register.
services.tab2Item1: Design of the LiDAR and/or photogrammetric flight plan • Plan for the differential GNSS ground control network survey (equipment, technique, observation times).
services.tab3Item1: Equipment deployment and GNSS network measurement. • LiDAR flights and simultaneous RGB image capture.
services.tab3Item2: Field validation of coverage and preliminary data quality.
services.tab4Item1: Office GNSS processing: network precision adjustment and validation.
services.tab4Item2: LiDAR processing: point cloud generation, semi-automatic and/or AI classification and manual non-conformity control.
services.tab4Item3: Orthomosaic: radiometric adjustment and uniformity control.
services.tab4Item4: DEM/DTM: tile generation with absolute and relative quality reports.
services.tab5Item1: Detailed description of methods, QC results, metadata, and lessons learned.
services.tab5Item2: Delivery of all products on external drives with a backup copy.

faqs.q1Parrafo1: Geomatics is the discipline that integrates the acquisition, processing, analysis, visualization and storage of geospatial data, i.e., information that references a location on Earth. In essence, it combines Earth sciences with information and communication technologies (ICT).
faqs.q1Parrafo2: This discipline groups a set of technologies and sciences that allow the capture, analysis, and interpretation of territorial information. Its main objective is geographic knowledge for better decision-making across a wide variety of fields.
faqs.q1SIGDesc: Specialized software to create, manage, analyze, and visualize geographic data on maps.
faqs.q1TDDesc: Acquisition of information about the Earth's surface from sensors installed on satellites or aircraft.
faqs.q1GNSSDesc: Such as GPS, providing precise positioning anywhere on the planet.
faqs.q1TopoDesc: Sciences that study the graphical representation of the Earth's surface and its shape and dimensions.
faqs.q1FotoDesc: Technique for obtaining maps and precise measurements from aerial or ground photographs.
faqs.q1CartoDesc: The art and science of creating maps and other graphical representations of geographic information.
faqs.q2Respuesta:
The LiDAR system is especially effective for generating high-precision and detailed topographic maps, as well as for measuring heights and other environmental features. Its main advantage lies in the laser scanner's ability to penetrate vegetation foliage, allowing it to capture terrain points hidden under tree cover.

Photogrammetry, on the other hand, is based on the analysis of digital images to build two- or three-dimensional models. This technique is particularly valuable for generating textured maps and visually representing complex structures and surfaces.

Both technologies not only complement each other, but when integrated, they enhance the quality and richness of the information obtained. The choice between one or the other, or the combination of both, will depend on the specific objectives of the project and the available resources.
faqs.q3Titulo: What is LiDAR technology and how does it work?
faqs.q3Respuesta:
LiDAR (Light Detection and Ranging) is a remote sensing technology that uses laser pulses to measure distances with great precision. By emitting thousands of pulses per second from a sensor mounted on an aircraft, and recording the time it takes for each one to return after hitting a surface, it is possible to generate very accurate three-dimensional models of the terrain, vegetation, and man-made structures.

Each of these distances is transformed into a spatial coordinate (X,Y,Z) plus certain attributes to generate a 3D model.

Today, LiDAR technology is the best remote sensing method for precision topographic surveys anywhere in the world.
faqs.q4Titulo: What advantages does LiDAR have over traditional survey methods?
faqs.q4Item2: It is the only technology capable of penetrating vegetation and obtaining terrain data under forest cover.
faqs.q4Item3: Centimetric precision in elevation and distance.
faqs.q5Titulo: What precision can be achieved with a LiDAR survey?
faqs.q5Respuesta: With an appropriate flight design and differential GNSS control, it is possible to achieve vertical precision of 2 to 10 cm and horizontal precision of 5 to 15 cm, even in complex areas. These precisions are validated using ground control points (GCPs) and checkpoints on the ground.
faqs.q6Titulo: What is the minimum and maximum area that can be surveyed with a helicopter?
faqs.q6Respuesta: There is no strict technical restriction, however from an operational efficiency standpoint, we recommend minimum areas of 1,000 hectares and maximum areas of 60,000 hectares. This covers the majority of cities in Latin America.
faqs.q7Titulo: What weather conditions affect data capture?
faqs.q7Item1: LiDAR can operate under cloudy conditions, variable light, and in complete darkness, as it does not depend on solar illumination.
faqs.q7Item2: Photogrammetry requires clear skies and good lighting to achieve consistent images and high-quality orthomosaics.
faqs.q8Titulo: What sensors does JASFLY use for its surveys?
faqs.q8Parrafo1: We use a RIEGL VUX-1LR-22 scanner, specifically designed for helicopters, which allows us to capture high-density and high-precision data. This system is complemented by two cameras, one RGB and one 10-band high-resolution Multispectral, synchronized with the inertial and GNSS navigation system.
faqs.q8Parrafo2: What applications can LiDAR be used for?
faqs.q8Parrafo3: Among the most important applications, the following can be mentioned:
faqs.q8App1: Urban development and planning, infrastructure monitoring (Smart Cities)
faqs.q8App7: Forestry: structure and composition of forests, including tree height, density, and species distribution.
faqs.q8App12: Coastal Monitoring: mapping of coastal area topography, including beaches, dunes, and wetlands.
faqs.q8App13: Dynamic models: flood, shadow, wind studies, etc.

projectDetails.p1Parrafo1: The main objective of this project was to survey the city of Bahía Blanca, in the Province of Buenos Aires, using LiDAR technology and aerial photogrammetry, in order to generate a high-precision Digital Elevation Model (DEM), along with an RGB image orthomosaic. These products enable a detailed representation of the terrain relief and surface characteristics, constituting fundamental tools for urban planning and land management.
projectDetails.p1Parrafo2: The surveyed area covered more than 20,000 hectares, including both urban sectors and a stretch of the Napostá stream, located northeast of the city.
projectDetails.p2Parrafo1: In March 2025, JASFLY S.A. successfully carried out an aerial LiDAR survey at Cerro Catedral, Bariloche, to generate a high-precision Digital Terrain Model (DTM). This data enables calibration of snow measurement systems and planning of winter operations, optimizing resources and anticipating terrain changes.
projectDetails.p2Parrafo2: Cerro Catedral, Argentina's main ski resort (and one of the most recognized in South America), requires precise topographic data for its management and development. This project was JASFLY's first venture into LiDAR surveys in mountainous areas, consolidating its national leadership and opening opportunities in mining, infrastructure, and geotechnical studies.
projectDetails.p3Parrafo1: The generation of a Digital Twin of the city of La Plata is underway, which will become an incredibly powerful tool for management and planning. This three-dimensional model will not only improve communication within public administration and with citizens, but will help visualize and communicate projects more effectively, enabling more informed and collaborative decisions to ensure sustainable urban development.
projectDetails.p3Parrafo2: It will also provide detailed information for the analysis of urban vegetation, the number and distribution of paved streets, together with the city's cadastre. The Digital Elevation Model (DEM) will enable flood simulations that have long affected the city.
projectDetails.p4Parrafo1: The project aimed to generate a Digital Elevation Model (DEM) to analyze height variations in the Pilcomayo River area, facilitating studies on floods, infrastructure, and environmental impact. A LiDAR and Photogrammetric survey was carried out over 137,000 hectares in the Province of Formosa, through 12 eight-hour flights. The remote location and logistical challenges demanded rigorous planning and precise execution.
projectDetails.p4Parrafo2: Thanks to a highly trained team, operational difficulties were successfully overcome. In addition, a local geoid model was developed to reference the DEM to sea level, improving the precision of the data obtained.
projectDetails.p5Parrafo1: The surveyed area covered 15,000 hectares along the natural course of the Neuquén River. From the LiDAR survey, a high-precision Digital Elevation Model was obtained, complemented by RGB and multispectral photographic records with centimetric resolution.
projectDetails.p5Parrafo2: The integration of this data enables advanced analysis of river dynamics, detection of vegetation changes, and monitoring of infrastructure near the riverbed. It also generates highly valuable cartographic inputs for land-use planning, project design, and water resource management, providing a comprehensive view of the studied area.
projectDetails.p6Parrafo1: A LiDAR survey was carried out over 53 kilometers following the railway track in the City of Buenos Aires, from Retiro station to the town of Pilar. The aerial survey produced a high-precision three-dimensional model of the rail corridor and its immediate surroundings, incorporating both the terrain geometry and existing infrastructure.
projectDetails.p6Parrafo2: The information obtained will be used as a fundamental input for the design and planning of a Metrobus-type transport corridor parallel to the railway line. Thanks to the point density and metric accuracy of LiDAR, it will be possible to evaluate route alternatives, analyze interference with existing urban infrastructure, optimize the geometric design of the corridor, and facilitate the subsequent detailed engineering stage.
